title: EMI Reality Check
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you ensure EMI payments enhance rather than hinder your financial goals?
What strategies can you use to maintain financial flexibility while managing loan obligations?

options.label: As high as possible
options.reflection: Taking on high EMIs can severely limit your financial flexibility and increase stress. It's important to choose EMIs that are manageable within your income.

options.label: The minimum amount to get the loan
options.reflection: While minimum EMIs might seem attractive, they often result in longer repayment periods and higher total interest costs. Balance is key.

options.label: Whatever the lender offers
options.reflection: Blindly accepting lender terms without considering your financial capacity can lead to financial distress. Always assess affordability first.

options.label: Comfortable within income
options.reflection: Exactly! EMI payments should be comfortably affordable within your income, leaving room for other expenses and savings. This ensures financial stability.

stages.prompt: What happens when you take high EMIs?

options.label: You get more financial freedom
options.reflection: High EMIs actually reduce your financial freedom by limiting your ability to save, invest, or handle unexpected expenses. They create financial pressure.

options.label: You face stress during repayment
options.reflection: Perfect! High EMIs can create significant financial stress, especially if your income fluctuates or unexpected expenses arise.

options.label: You get better loan benefits
options.reflection: Higher EMIs don't necessarily mean better loan benefits. They primarily mean faster repayment but also higher monthly financial commitment.

options.label: Your credit score improves faster
options.reflection: While timely EMI payments do help credit scores, taking on unaffordable EMIs can actually harm your credit if you struggle to make payments.

stages.prompt: How should you calculate your EMI affordability?

options.label: Based on your total monthly income
options.reflection: Using total income without considering other expenses can lead to overcommitment. You need to account for all your financial obligations and savings goals.

options.label: What your friends are paying
options.reflection: Everyone's financial situation is different. What's affordable for others may not be for you. Base your decisions on your own financial capacity.

options.label: Based on your disposable income after expenses
options.reflection: Exactly! Calculate EMIs based on what you can afford after covering essential expenses and setting aside some savings. This ensures sustainability.

options.label: The maximum amount the lender approves
options.reflection: Lender approval doesn't guarantee affordability. Just because you're approved for a high EMI doesn't mean you should take it on.

stages.prompt: What is a good EMI-to-income ratio?

options.label: Ideally 20% or less of monthly income
options.reflection: Perfect! Keeping EMIs at 20% or less of your income ensures you maintain financial flexibility and can handle emergencies or other goals.

options.label: Up to 50% of monthly income
options.reflection: 50% is too high and can severely limit your financial flexibility. This leaves little room for savings or unexpected expenses.

options.label: Around 30% of monthly income
options.reflection: 30% is on the higher side and may strain your finances. It's better to stay closer to 20% for better financial health.

options.label: It doesn't matter as long as I can pay
options.reflection: The EMI amount does matter for your overall financial health. Even if you can pay, high EMIs reduce your financial flexibility and increase risk.

stages.prompt: Before taking a loan, you should:

options.label: Apply first and see if you can manage
options.reflection: This approach is risky. You should assess affordability before applying to avoid getting into a financial commitment you can't handle.

options.label: Compare interest rates only
options.reflection: While interest rates are important, they're not the only factor. Affordability and your ability to manage the EMI are equally crucial.

options.label: Take it if you need it urgently
options.reflection: Urgent needs shouldn't override financial planning. Even urgent needs should be addressed within your financial capacity to avoid long-term problems.

options.label: Calculate if EMI fits your budget comfortably
options.reflection: Exactly! Always calculate and ensure the EMI fits comfortably within your budget before taking any loan. This prevents future financial stress.
